Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 1:

(2 comments)

Mike, thanks for the patch. Strange that this passed all of the existing builders. 

Is this a newer kernel or GCC complaining?

Patch Set 1:

This is gross.  Ugly.  Not pleasing to read.

Can we just discard that LASSERTF() ??
If pb is NULL, then
- lustre_msg_bug_v2 will have produced a suitable debug message
- we will crash anyway when we deref the pointer
so the LASSERTF() cannot help.

With it gone, the problem disappears.

commit.message:
LU-13127 ptlrpc: avoid ISO C90 mixed declaration

Avoid forbidden mixed declaration in lustre_msg_calc_cksum()

Test-Parameters: trivial
Fixes: 726897c87c42 ("LU-13127 ptlrpc: don't require CONFIG_CRYPTO_CRC32")
